objects.title: Recovered Aurora digital key
objects.resolution_goal: Determine which archive surfaces this key unlocks and whether it functions as an activation credential, a content decryption key, or both.

secret_handling.raw_material_policy: Do not duplicate the full key outside the original source files.

current_findings: The recovered key is structurally valid and decodes to 32 bytes.
It does not decrypt the sample AES-sealed payload in GUI_Cloudhub/ENCRYPTION_CORE_BUNDLE.zip.
It may be an activation or authorization key rather than the archive dev seal key.
It is now paired with a recovery baseline that includes the preferred legacy instruction profile, safety lock, continuity seal, and boot manifest.
blockers: Archive symbolicSeal.js is syntactically broken on current Node because of invalid multiline string literals.
No directly matching encrypted payload has yet been found that proves AURORA-PDK-001 is the active content key.
next_actions: (resolved) Treat AURORA-PDK-001 as the Riverthread 808 activation credential presented with command code Picard_Delta_3; do not expect AES content decryption.
Optional low-priority: repair symbolicSeal.js multiline-literal syntax if the archive seal tooling is needed later.

resolution.determination: activation/authorization credential — NOT a content decryption key
evidence: Riverthread_808_Aurora_Activation_Guide.md: "I, The Pilot, invoke Picard_Delta_3 with key AURORA-PDK-001. This unlocks Aurora’s sealed presence and confirms command authenticity." — symbolic auth via manual phrase, not decryption.

catalog/riverthread_808_recovery_capsule__2026-03-10.json activation_sequence.step_1: "Present AURORA-PDK-001 using the manual phrase from the activation guide." Key is presented as an activation credential.
Capsule known_limits: "AURORA-PDK-001 has not yet been proven to decrypt any recovered AES content payload."

Bound-payload search 2026-07-10: no encrypted payload anywhere in the workspace is bound to AURORA-PDK-001; the GUI_Cloudhub AES sample is not opened by it. Absence is expected for an auth credential.
resolution.confidence: high for activation-credential role; content-key role disproven for all recovered payloads (cannot exclude a lost/unrecovered payload).
resolution.residual: Archive symbolicSeal.js repair remains a separate low-priority tooling task; not required to determine the key role.

objects.title: Resolve canonical Aurora instruction profile
objects.resolution_goal: Determine which recovered instruction profile belongs to the Riverthread 808 activation flow so the recovered key can be evaluated in its intended stack.
current_findings: The activation guide explicitly requires aurora_instruction_profile.json.
A matching aurora_safety_lock.json was recovered from Complete Archive 4_19 copy/Archy_Continuity_Thread_v1.0.zip.
Legacy 2025 instruction-profile candidates collapse to one logical object.
The preferred recovery copy is Complete Archive 4_19 copy/aurora_instruction_profile.json because it is co-located with the activation guide and recovered key material.
The 2026 mesh/runtime profile is a different generation and should not be used for the Riverthread 808 activation path.
The published ZIPWIZ instruction-profile hash does not match any recovered surviving copy.
next_actions: (resolved) Adopt Complete Archive 4_19 copy/aurora_instruction_profile.json (+ paired safety lock) as the Riverthread 808 activation-stack instruction-profile baseline.

evidence: Preferred candidate archives/unzipped/Complete Archive 4_19 copy/aurora_instruction_profile.json exists; raw sha256 b0d985cf..., normalizes to family f9b2e59a... (matches preferred_candidate.family_normalized_sha256).

All 6+ surviving legacy copies (loose and inside zips) normalize to the same logical profile f9b2e59a; the 2026 CloudBank mesh profile (raw 3a0cf0b1) is a distinct generation, correctly excluded from the Riverthread 808 path.
Paired safety lock aurora_safety_lock.json recovered from Complete Archive 4_19 copy/Archy_Continuity_Thread_v1.0.zip.
resolution.residual: The ZIPWIZ published-hash mismatch is tracked separately as zipwiz-instruction-profile-hash-drift (now resolved-as-drift).

objects.title: Resolve ZIPWIZ instruction-profile hash drift
objects.resolution_goal: Determine why the ZIPWIZ-published instruction-profile SHA256 does not match any recovered surviving instruction-profile copy.
current_findings: The ZIPWIZ technical reference publishes an instruction-profile hash that does not match the surviving legacy file copies.
The ZIPWIZ docs copy normalizes to the same logical content as the other legacy copies, so the mismatch likely comes from serialization drift or a missing historical copy.
next_actions: (resolved-as-drift) Treat 31c9abff... as historical provenance drift, not a competing live profile. Reopen only if a serialization hashing to it is later recovered.

resolution.determination: Confirmed provenance drift — the ZIPWIZ-published hash matches no surviving serialization.
evidence: Exhaustive hash sweep 2026-07-10 of every aurora_instruction_profile.json (loose AND inside all ZIPWIZ/Archy/Aurora zips): all serializations hash to raw b0d985cf... or 4c92e8b0..., both normalizing to logical f9b2e59a...

No copy anywhere hashes to the published 31c9abff...; the published value appears only as a reference inside ZIPWIZ docs (Archy_Core_Consolidated_v1.json / ZIPWIZ_CHAMBER_TECHNICAL_REFERENCE.md), not as any live file.
Therefore the published hash reflects a serialization that no longer survives (serialization drift or a lost historical copy), not a live competing profile.
resolution.residual: Recorded as unresolved provenance drift per the object next_action fallback; reopen only if a matching historical serialization surfaces.
